cards.title: Bug Triage
Incident Command
Focus Inbox
Release Cutline
Sprint Board

package.name: kanban.v1
package.title: Kanban Runtime Pack
package.description: Structured Kanban shell runtime pack for HyperCard VM-authored cards.
package.prose:
The Kanban runtime pack exposes a compositional page contract. A VM card composes
widgets.kanban.page(...) from smaller semantic widgets such as taxonomy, header, optional
highlights, optional filters, board, and optional status nodes. The VM remains responsible for
semantic state and handler wiring. The host remains responsible for rendering, drag/drop behavior,
and modal UI.

symbols.name: widgets.kanban.page
symbols.summary: Compose the root page for a Kanban runtime tree.
symbols.prose:
Use widgets.kanban.page(...) as the root return value from a kanban.v1 card render function.
Pass child nodes in a component-like style: taxonomy, header, optional highlights, optional
filters, board, and optional status nodes.

symbols.name: widgets.kanban.taxonomy
symbols.summary: Describe the available issue types, priorities, and labels for a Kanban shell.
symbols.prose:
Use widgets.kanban.taxonomy({...}) to register descriptor-driven issue systems. This is the main
replacement for hardcoded bug/feature/tag enums.

symbols.name: widgets.kanban.header
symbols.summary: Describe the shell header with title, subtitle, search state, and primary action wiring.
symbols.prose:
Use widgets.kanban.header({...}) to configure page title text, search query, and the primary
action button. Typical cards wire the primary action back to openTaskEditor.

symbols.name: widgets.kanban.highlights
symbols.summary: Describe a row of summary cards with metrics, captions, progress, or sparklines.
symbols.prose:
Use widgets.kanban.highlights({...}) to render summary cards above the board. This is the main
mechanism for making Kanban pages feel different without escaping the semantic DSL.

symbols.name: widgets.kanban.filters
symbols.summary: Describe the optional filter bar for issue-type and priority filtering.
symbols.prose:
Use widgets.kanban.filters({...}) when the board should expose issue-type and priority filters. If
the card omits this node, the host page will not render the filter bar.

symbols.name: widgets.kanban.board
symbols.summary: Describe the core board state, task list, lane list, editing state, and event bindings.
symbols.prose:
Use widgets.kanban.board({...}) to provide columns, tasks, collapsed state, editing state, and
board interaction handlers. This node is still semantic data, not a host component escape hatch.

symbols.name: widgets.kanban.status
symbols.summary: Describe optional footer metrics for the shell.
symbols.prose:
Use widgets.kanban.status({...}) when the page should render explicit footer metrics. Cards may
omit this node entirely, or provide custom metrics that match their own domain vocabulary.

symbols.name: kanbanBugTriage
symbols.summary: Four-lane bug triage desk with hot-issue highlights and a custom incident taxonomy.
symbols.prose:
This demo card uses the same board primitive but a different top section: a triage-oriented
highlight strip that calls out hot issues before the board itself.

symbols.name: kanbanIncidentCommand
symbols.summary: Three-lane incident command surface with telemetry highlights and custom status metrics.
symbols.prose:
This demo card shows the command-center end of the spectrum: telemetry highlights above a denser
three-lane board, still authored entirely with semantic Kanban primitives.

symbols.name: kanbanPersonalPlanner
symbols.summary: Single-lane focus inbox demo using the compositional kanban.v1 page DSL.
symbols.prose:
This demo card is intentionally minimal: one lane, no filter bar, and only a small focus-oriented
highlight strip above the board.

symbols.name: kanbanReleaseTrain
symbols.summary: Two-lane release cutline board with launch highlights and custom release taxonomy.
symbols.prose:
This demo card is intentionally narrow: just two lanes, no filters, and a small launch cutline
summary that makes the board feel very different from the sprint or incident examples.

symbols.name: kanbanSprintBoard
symbols.summary: Five-lane sprint radar demo for the compositional kanban.v1 page DSL.
symbols.prose:
This demo card uses the page-style DSL to build a denser sprint radar with an idea lane and a
summary highlight strip above the board.
